name: Agent Health Monitor
description: Checks all active agents in agent_registry for consecutive execution failures. Scans n8n execution logs per agent, counts consecutive errors, flags agents needing retirement (>=3 errors). Writes scan to experimental_outputs. Notifies via sandbox_notify if issues found.

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ agent_name: 'agent_health_monitor', experiment_id: 'health_scan_' + new Date().toISOString().slice(0,16).replace(/[T:]/g,'_'), output_type: 'health_scan', content: $json, confidence: 1.0 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ agent_name: 'agent_health_monitor', message: '🔴 Health scan: ' + $json.agents_with_issues + ' agent(s) with consecutive errors (' + $json.agents_retiring + ' need retirement). Issues: ' + $json.issues.map(i => i.agent_name + ' (' + i.consecutive_errors + ' errors)').join(', '), trigger_url: 'http://localhost:5678/webhook/agent-health-monitor' }) }}

notes: KEY LESSON: webhookId must be set at node level (top-level field), not inside parameters, for n8n to register the webhook route.
KEY LESSON: runOnceForAllItems Code nodes do NOT fan out to downstream per-item Code nodes in n8n 2.6.4. Combined Merge+Analyze into single runOnceForAllItems node to work around this.
KEY LESSON: Use contentType=raw + JSON.stringify for complex nested POSTs to Supabase. specifyBody=json fails with nested objects.
First scan result (2026-03-30): 9 agents checked, 1 issue (telegram_command_agent: 1 consecutive error), 0 retiring.
